rationale: The citing paper repeatedly uses Homburg et al. (2014) as the key prior marketing study on VC seed funding, including in the introduction, literature review, and contribution framing. It explicitly states that Homburg et al. opened the question and then positions the current study as a complement by shifting from CMO human capital to trademark applications as an early marketing action. The paper also returns to Homburg et al. in the discussion to interpret similarities and differences in findings, indicating a meaningful conceptual role rather than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing study builds directly on Homburg et al. (2014)'s marketing-legitimacy line of work in VC seed funding and extends it from manager attributes (CMO human capital) to firm actions (trademark applications). It adds temporal effects and contextual moderators while positioning its contribution as complementary to that earlier study.
